title: Dried plasma spot biobanking protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"kh1og","text":"This protocol presents methods for collection and biobanking of plasma in the form of a dried plasma spot (DPS) from whole blood drawn by standard phlebotomy. Leveraging Capitainer SEP10 cards, for a shelf stable, consistent 10 ul DPS, this protocol may be inserted in the beginning of any workflow involving fresh anticoagulated whole blood, such as isolation of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC), in which only 75-150 ul may be available to spare. Resulting DPS are held at room temperature until elution for downstream applications enabling a more flexible workflow compared to standard plasma biobanking methods. Accurate quantification of analytes for proteomic assessment may be achieved by correcting through a dilution factor, considering (i) phlebotomy tube anticoagulant volume and (ii) total volume whole blood drawn. This protocol supplements original work published by Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"te5m1","text":"8.5 ml acid-citrate-dextrose (ACD) or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) phlebotomy tube (whole blood drawn)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uquxr","text":"Capitainer® SEP10 dried blood spot device, Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yobxe","text":"P200 wide bore tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"adhg3","text":"P200 mechanical pipette","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"samov","text":"Tissue culture biosafety cabinet","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jrg5r","text":"Storage box lid (9x9)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rmiw","text":"50 ml conical tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtain whole blood drawn into ACD or EDTA phlebotomy tube.
Clean and sanitize tissue culture hood with 70% ethanol Contributed by users , wipe down with Kimwipe, and UV sterilize.
Allow biospecimen to come to Room temperature ( 00:30:00 post-draw) and briefly spin to remove residual whole blood from the top of tube.
Spray phlebotomy tube, sealed Capitainer SEP10 sampling card pack Contributed by users , and 9x9 box lid into tissue culture hood with 70% ethanol Contributed by users .
Transfer total volume of whole blood into labeled 50 mL conical with 10 mL serological pipette tip to ensure whole blood is uniform prior to sampling.
Note total volume of whole blood.
Proceed with PBMC isolation or other primary phlebotomy tube application using the remaining whole blood volume.
Open the Capitainer SEP10 sampling card pack Contributed by users in the tissue culture hood. Use within 00:30:00 of opening.
Label card with relevant project info prior to sampling on top card lid. Use gentle pressure to maintain integrity of fragile sampling channels.
Fold back the lid of the card to reveal two sampling lanes. Do not touch the sample inlets.
Gently place card on the edge of the 9x9 box lid to give sampling lanes a lift to ease sampling.
Slowly and smoothly pipette 75 µL of whole blood into one card lane until the sample indicator line has been filled. There should be 5 µL, (2-5 ul) whole blood remaining in tip.
Repeat for the second card lane.
Immediately tilt card at 90° angle for two seconds before lying flat.
Keep card flat and under observation for 00:10:00 until control window appears blue to indicate successful sampling. Upon successful sampling, blue color indicator will be faint.
Close card lid flat. Label with any additional study info and tape closed for storage at Room temperature .
For standard SEP10 sampling methods, please see Capitainer AB, Uppsala, Sweden, Capitainer‱ SEP10 dried blood spot device: Instructions for Use, 2025. Available from: Manufacturer website.
Record all observations and storage locations in secure spreadsheet or LIMS databasesystem
creator: Taylor Johnson
